mechanism_check: Partially implemented. GaussianPyramid and HFNoiseGate exist, but the code deviates by substituting ResNet18 for the hypothesized EfficientViT-M4/ConvNeXt-Tiny. Crucial mechanisms like Patch Splitter/Merger, FAISS retrieval, and Consensus Aggregation are entirely missing due to truncation.
spec_alignment: Misaligned in key areas. The spec mandates EfficientViT-M4, but the code uses ResNet18. The GaussianPyramid accepts a 'levels=2' argument but only performs a 1-level separation in its forward pass. Most specified components (FAISS, Patch Merger) are absent.
metric_evaluation_path: Not implemented. The provided code snippet contains only partial model definitions and lacks any training, evaluation, or metric computation logic to calculate pixel AUROC or FPR.
constraints_compliance: Freezing the encoder respects the lightweight/single-GPU constraint. However, compliance with the <60s inference time and memory limits cannot be verified without the patch batching and FAISS retrieval implementation for the ~1225 patches.

issues.description: The code is truncated mid-class (AdaptiveInstanceNorm) and lacks the core pipeline integrating the components.
issues.design_suggestion: Complete the implementation of AdaIN, and implement the main anomaly detection pipeline linking the Patch Splitter, Encoder, Retriever, Scorer, and Patch Merger.

issues.description: Backbone substitution: Code uses ResNet18 instead of the spec-mandated EfficientViT-M4.
issues.design_suggestion: Replace ResNet18 with EfficientViT-M4 (or ConvNeXt-Tiny) to align with the hypothesis and parameter budget constraints.

issues.description: GaussianPyramid does not utilize the 'levels' parameter, performing only a single-level separation.
issues.design_suggestion: Implement an iterative or recursive decomposition in GaussianPyramid to actually extract multi-level LF and HF features if 'levels > 1' is required by the mechanism.

missing_components: Consensus Aggregator (element-wise median of K residuals)
bottlenecks: Processing ~1225 patches through the encoder per image and querying the FAISS index for each patch at multiple scales may bottleneck the <60s inference constraint if strict batching and GPU acceleration for FAISS are not properly configured.
overall_comment: The foundational components for frequency decomposition and HF gating are conceptually aligned but architecturally deviated (ResNet18 vs EfficientViT). The code is severely truncated, leaving the majority of the hypothesized mechanism unverified. The missing pipeline, FAISS integration, and patch merging logic must be implemented to fulfill the experiment contract.
